title: Nested macro expansion not evaluating correctly
body:
I'm trying to get a design from one of the commercial simulators / synthesis tools to run with iverilog and running into a macro expansion issue with ivlpp (version 10.1).  The verilog code uses nested macro expansion which expands a single macro out to nearly 1000 signals.  I have included a much smaller snippet here:

```verilog
`define IO_HLPR_INPUT_2_VEC (name,c) \
   input [c-1:0] name``_0, \
   input [c-1:0] name``_1,

`define IO_HLPR_OUTPUT_2_VEC (n,c) \
   output [c-1:0] n``_0, \
   output [c-1:0] n``_1,

`define ADC_IO_SIGNALS(prefix)\
   `IO_HLPR_INPUT_2_VEC(prefix``bits,6) \
   `IO_HLPR_OUTPUT_2_VEC(prefix``data,10) \
   input prefix``clk

module adc_dsp
(
   `ADC_IO_SIGNALS(adc1_)
);
endmodule
```

This *should* evaluate to the following:

```verilog
module adc_dsp
(
   input [6-1:0] adc1_bits_0,
   input [6-1:0] adc1_bits_1,
   output [10-1:0] adc1_data_0,
   output [10-1:0] adc1_data_1,
   input adc1_clk
);
endmodule
```

However when I issue the command ivlpp macro.v (my test filename), the generated output after the macro expansion is:

```verilog
module adc_dsp
(
   
   (name,c)
   input [c-1:0] name_0,
   input [c-1:0] name_1,(adc1_bits,6)
   (n,c)
   output [c-1:0] n_0,
   output [c-1:0] n_1,(adc1_data,10)
   input adc1_clk
);
endmodule
```

Any assistance resolving this would be a great help and appreciated.  
Thanks,
Ken
code_blocks:
`define IO_HLPR_INPUT_2_VEC (name,c) \
   input [c-1:0] name``_0, \
   input [c-1:0] name``_1,

`define IO_HLPR_OUTPUT_2_VEC (n,c) \
   output [c-1:0] n``_0, \
   output [c-1:0] n``_1,

`define ADC_IO_SIGNALS(prefix)\
   `IO_HLPR_INPUT_2_VEC(prefix``bits,6) \
   `IO_HLPR_OUTPUT_2_VEC(prefix``data,10) \
   input prefix``clk

module adc_dsp
(
   `ADC_IO_SIGNALS(adc1_)
);
endmodule

module adc_dsp
(
   input [6-1:0] adc1_bits_0,
   input [6-1:0] adc1_bits_1,
   output [10-1:0] adc1_data_0,
   output [10-1:0] adc1_data_1,
   input adc1_clk
);
endmodule

module adc_dsp
(
   
   (name,c)
   input [c-1:0] name_0,
   input [c-1:0] name_1,(adc1_bits,6)
   (n,c)
   output [c-1:0] n_0,
   output [c-1:0] n_1,(adc1_data,10)
   input adc1_clk
);
endmodule
